sections.label: Section 1464
sections.original: καὶ δὴ τελεῖται τἀπʼ ἐμοῦ · τῷ γὰρ χρόνῳ
sections.translation: All will be done on my part . Time

sections.label: Section 1465
sections.original: νοῦν ἔσχον , ὥστε συμφέρειν τοῖς κρείσσοσιν .
sections.translation: has given me the sense to comply with the stronger .

sections.label: Section 1466
sections.original: ὦ Ζεῦ , δέδορκα φάσμʼ ἄνευ φθόνου μὲν οὐ
sections.translation: O Zeus , I see an image which could not have fallen without divine spite — but , if Nemesis attend what I say , let it be unsaid ! To Orestes . Undo the coverings from his eyes , so that our kinship , at least , may receive due mourning from me also .

sections.label: Section 1470
sections.original: αὐτὸς σὺ βάσταζʼ · οὐκ ἐμὸν τόδʼ , ἀλλὰ σόν ,
sections.translation: Lift the veil yourself . It is not for me , but for you to look upon these remains and greet them kindly .

sections.label: Section 1472
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εὖ παραινεῖς κἀπιπείσομαι · σὺ δέ ,
sections.translation: You advise well , and I will obey you . To Electra . But you , call Clytaemnestra for me , if she is at home .

sections.label: Section 1474
sections.original: αὕτη πέλας σοῦ · μηκέτʼ ἄλλοσε σκόπει .
sections.translation: She is near you ; do not look elsewhere .

sections.label: Section 1475
sections.original: οἴμοι , τί λεύσσω ;
sections.translation: O , what sight is this !

sections.label: Section 1476
sections.original: τίνα φοβεῖ ; τίνʼ ἀγνοεῖς ;
sections.translation: Who are the men into whose nets I have miserably fallen ?

sections.label: Section 1479
sections.original: ζῶντας θανοῦσιν οὕνεκʼ ἀνταυδᾷς ἴσα ;
sections.translation: Ah , I read the riddle ! It cannot be that

sections.label: Section 1480
sections.original: οἴμοι , ξυνῆκα τοὔπος · οὐ γὰρ ἔσθʼ ὅπως
sections.translation: this is not Orestes who speaks to me !

sections.label: Section 1481
sections.original: ὅδʼ οὐκ Ὀρέστης ἔσθʼ ὁ προσφωνῶν ἐμέ .
sections.translation: And , though so good a prophet , were you deceived so long ?

sections.label: Section 1482
sections.original: καὶ μάντις ὢν ἄριστος ἐσφάλλου πάλαι .
sections.translation: Oh , I am destroyed , undone ! Yet allow me to speak just a little .

sections.label: Section 1485
sections.original: πρὸς θεῶν , ἀδελφέ , μηδὲ μηκύνειν λόγους .
sections.translation: When mortals are embroiled in misfortunes , how can one who is to die benefit from lapse of time ? No , kill him as quickly as you can , and throw his corpse to the creatures from whom his kind should have burial , throw it far from our sight ! For in my eyes this

sections.label: Section 1490
sections.original: ἄποπτον ἡμῶν · ὡς ἐμοὶ τόδʼ ἂν κακῶν
sections.translation: alone can bring us release from the misery of the past .

sections.label: Section 1491
sections.original: χωροῖς ἂν εἴσω σὺν τάχει · λόγων γὰρ οὐ
sections.translation: To Aegisthus . Go in , and quickly . Words are not at stake here , but your life .

sections.label: Section 1493
sections.original: τί δʼ ἐς δόμους ἄγεις με ; πῶς , τόδʼ εἰ καλὸν
sections.translation: Why take me into the house ? If this deed is just , what need is there of darkness ? Why is your hand not quick to strike ?

sections.label: Section 1495
sections.original: μὴ τάσσε · χώρει δʼ ἔνθαπερ κατέκτανες
sections.translation: Do not give orders , but go to where you struck down my father , so that in that very place you may die .

sections.label: Section 1497
sections.original: ἦ πᾶσʼ ἀνάγκη τήνδε τὴν στέγην ἰδεῖν
sections.translation: Is this dwelling doomed to see all the sufferings of us descendants of Pelops , both now and in time to come ?

sections.label: Section 1499
sections.original: τὰ γοῦν σʼ · ἐγώ σοι μάντις εἰμὶ τῶνδʼ ἄκρος .
sections.translation: Yours , at least . I am for you a consummate prophet in these matters .

sections.label: Section 1500
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐ πατρῴαν τὴν τέχνην ἐκόμπασας .
sections.translation: The skill you boast about did not belong to your father .

sections.label: Section 1501
sections.original: πόλλʼ ἀντιφωνεῖς , ἡ δʼ ὁδὸς βραδύνεται .
sections.translation: You bandy words , and our going is delayed . Move forward !

sections.label: Section 1502a
sections.original: ὑφηγοῦ .
sections.translation: You lead .

sections.label: Section 1502b
sections.original: σοὶ βαδιστέον πάρος .
sections.translation: You must go first .

sections.label: Section 1503
sections.original: ἦ μὴ φύγω σε ;
sections.translation: Lest I escape you ?

sections.label: Section 1503a
sections.original: μὴ μὲν οὖν καθʼ ἡδονὴν
sections.translation: No , lest you die in a manner which pleases you . I must be sure that your death is bitter for you .

sections.label: Section 1505
sections.original: χρῆν δʼ εὐθὺς εἶναι τήνδε τοῖς πᾶσιν δίκην ,
sections.translation: This just penalty ought to come straightaway upon all who would break the laws : the penalty of death . Then wrongdoing would not abound .

sections.label: Section 1508
sections.original: ὦ σπέρμʼ Ἀτρέως , ὡς πολλὰ παθὸν
sections.translation: O seed of Atreus , through how many sufferings have you sprouted up at last in freedom ,

sections.label: Section 1510
sections.original: τῇ νῦν ὁρμῇ τελεωθέν .
sections.translation: fulfilled by this day ’ s enterprise !
